Empires & Puzzles Faline Review: The Acrobat's Last Stand?
Once the queen of speed and mana control, Faline now faces a world of 1600+ Attack stats. Can her -64% mana generation debuff still save the day, or has the circus left town without her?
Faline: Astonishing Acrobat or Yesterday's News?
Faline: The "Fastest" Mana Controller in the West (er, Circus)
Faline is a 5-star Holy hero from the

event. Released back in the golden days of 2021, she was once feared for her ability to shut down entire defenses with a single Chakram Kick. As a Fast speed Wizard, she specialized in punishing speedsters. Today, she’s a vintage classic—like a flip phone that still makes calls but can't run TikTok.

Faline’s design is top-tier circus aesthetic. She’s captured mid-handstand, defying gravity with a smug expression that says, "I bet you can't do this." The vibrant purples and star motifs fit the Starfall theme perfectly. It’s a dynamic, energetic pose that stands out even among the flashy animations of newer heroes.
Her base attack of ~784 (and even LB2 ~967) is now lower than many modern 4-star heroes.
With ~1394 HP and ~751 Defense, she is extremely fragile by 2026 standards. Modern snipers can often one-shot her without buffs.
Her Power rating of 804 is a relic. Modern 5-stars sit comfortably above 1260.
She relies entirely on her special skill's utility because she simply cannot trade blows with the stat monsters of today.
has two components: damage and mana control.
At Fast speed, this used to be devastating. With her current stats, it's more of a "firm nudge." It won't wipe teams anymore, but it can soften targets.
This is the crown jewel. -64% is a massive debuff. It essentially freezes the mana gain of the affected heroes. The unique mechanic targets the
In the modern meta, almost everyone is Fast or Very Fast. This means you can often land this debuff on 3 key threats simultaneously if you aim well.
Fast (requires 8 tiles, or 7 with a level 29 mana troop / lesser troop + emblem node).
Base Attack (784) x 330% = ~2,587 raw damage value.
A modern hero with 1300 attack doing 330% yields ~4,290 raw damage value. Faline hits about
to charge. A Fast hero needing 8 tiles would suddenly need ~22 tiles. It is a complete shutdown.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Starfall family bonus (40%/60% chance to cleanse) is excellent. Passive cleansing is always valuable, especially in bloody battles or against ailment-heavy teams. It makes her surprisingly annoying if she survives.
Faline does not have modern passives (like "start with 20% mana" or "resist ailments"). This is a significant disadvantage compared to newer heroes who often come with 2-3 innate abilities.
is... fine. It helps patch her low attack stat slightly, but "Damage Reduction" or "Dodge" would have been better to help her survive long enough to fire.
Upon release (and subsequent buff), Faline was a premier offensive hero. She was a staple in top-tier defenses and a key answer to the "Frigg/Odin" meta.
She has been crept hard. Her damage is negligible against modern defense stats (1600+ Defense). However, her utility (-64% mana gen) remains unique and powerful.
She is no longer viable in top 100 alliance wars. However, for mid-game players or in specific 5-star tournaments (Rush Attack), she can still ruin an attacker's day if she fires.
Maximizes her only source of damage. She needs every point of attack to make her hit 3 noticeable.
Keeps her alive longer to cast the mana debuff, which is her main utility now.
. If you are using Faline in 2026, you are likely using her for a specific offensive synergy where you need to soften up targets. If she's on defense, she's dead anyway.
8 tiles is still a sweet spot for offensive raiding.
Adds a layer of passive support.
Wizard talent (Jinx) can deal extra damage against buff-heavy teams.
Her biggest enemy. She hits like a wet noodle compared to modern heroes.
extremely low defense/HP makes her easy prey for modern snipers.
Her mana ailment is cleansable (unlike some modern permanent effects).
You cannot choose exactly who gets the mana debuff if multiple speeds are present; the AI decides based on "Fastest".
Everyone is Very Fast, so her skill targets everyone she hits. The mana cut is devastating here.
Good for taking out weakened teams where her lower damage is sufficient.
Still a solid hero for players below the Diamond arena.
Costume Leonidas provides the Elemental Defense Down required to make Faline's damage relevant. Faline hits 3, and Joon finishes the primary target.
Combine Faline's mana generation drop with complete mana stops (like Proteus) to ensure the enemy never fires a single skill.
Any healer who cleanses (Ariel, Rigard) removes her primary utility immediately.
Heroes with stoneskin or damage reduction will laugh at her damage.
If you pulled Faline from the "Challenge Festival" or a lucky coin:
She is a fantastic hero for map stages and early raiding. Level her up, but be wary of spending Alpha Aethers (LB2) on her unless she is your absolutely best hero.

. Unless you have a specific nostalgia for the Starfall Circus or need a Wizard for class quests, she likely belongs on your bench. Her stats are simply too low to compete with the 2025/2026 monsters. Do not Limit Break 2 her.
When Faline was released, players debated if she was "OP" or just "Strong." The buff she received shortly after release cemented her as a meta-definer.
"She hits 3 targets super hard and she has the ability to place a powerful mana debuff... astonishing." —
She was originally designed to counter the Odin/Frigg defenses by punishing Fast heroes.

Faline is a beautiful reminder of a simpler time in Empires & Puzzles. While her acrobatic days in the top meta are over, her kit is mechanically sound. If she ever gets a costume that updates her stats to 2026 standards, she will be terrifying once again. Until then, keep her ticket stub as a souvenir.
Great utility, tragic stats. A star that has fallen.
Is Faline still worth your darts in 2026? We analyze her mana control, damage potential, and viability in the modern era of Empires & Puzzles.
